channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer advance to presidential runoff
item.description: Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election after conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González garnered enough votes Sunday to beat 14 other candidates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Richard Gere calls Trump a 'bully and a thug' at awards show
item.description: Hollywood star Richard Gere called President Trump a “bully” and a “thug” in his acceptance speech at the Academy of Cinematographic Arts and Sciences of Spain's Goya Awards on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump, South Africa in growing row over hotly contested land law, country's deals with US foes
item.description: Trump’s executive order cutting aid and offering refugee status to South Africa’s Afrikaner farmers has caused a stir in the African country after passage of a controversial land bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser Mike Waltz says that top administration officials are traveling to Europe this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Serbian protesters block key bridge, roads to mark 100 days since deadly canopy collapse
item.description: Serbia's striking university students have blocked a bridge in Belgrade and roads throughout the Balkan country to mark 100 days since the collapse of a concrete canopy at a rail station which killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Famine mostly averted but is a danger again if Gaza ceasefire collapses, UN humanitarian chief says
item.description: The United Nations humanitarian chief says famine has been mostly averted in Gaza as a surge of aid enters the territory during a fragile ceasefire, but he warns the threat could return quickly if the truce collapses.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dalai Lama's elder brother, who led several rounds of talks with China, dies at 97
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The elder brother of the Dalai Lama and former chairman of the Tibetan government-in-exile in India, Gyalo Thondup, who led several rounds of talks with China and worked with foreign governments for the Tibetan cause, has died. He was 97.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Days before Trump halted funding, an ex-Israeli hostage was held at UNRWA school in Gaza, she reveals
item.description: Former Israeli hostage Emily Damari said she was held in an UNRWA center in Gaza. Damari was recently released as part of the hostages for ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kosovo's governing party wins parliamentary election but without majority, preliminary results show
item.description: Preliminary results show that Prime Minister Albin Kurti’s party won Kosovo’s parliamentary election but without the majority needed to govern alone, as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled and foreign funding for one of Europe’s poorest countries is in question.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Released Thai hostages return to Bangkok after being held for over a year in Gaza
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Five Thai workers released after being held hostage for over a year in Gaza arrived in Bangkok on Sunday. Sarusak Rumnao, 32, Watchara Sriaoun, 33, Sathian Suwannakham, 35, Pongsak Thaenna, 36, and Bannawat Saethao, 27, were freed on Jan. 30 as part of an exchange arrangement.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Defaced Holocaust mural finds new home in Rome's Shoah Museum
item.description: Contemporary pop artist aleXsandro Palombo is speaking out after his murals showing Holocaust survivors were defaced multiple times in Italy in apparent acts of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump halts aid to South Africa citing new land law
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order formally halting all aid to South Africa and urged his Cabinet to come up with a resettlement program for Afrikaners who he claims "are victims of unjust racial discrimination,” citing the country’s new land law intended to combat the racist apartheid era.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New report by panel of experts claims killer nurse Lucy Letby is innocent
item.description: Convicted of killing seven babies and attempting to kill seven more, Lucy Letby's conviction is being questioned by a new report that claims she is a victim of a miscarriage of justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian spy ship caught fire off Syria's coast, officials say. Here's audio of its broadcasts
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The man is insistent: Our ship is in difficulty, so keep your distance, he instructs another vessel over the radio. “Warship on your course,” he says. "I am drifting. I’m not under command.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages as part of ceasefire agreement with Israel
item.description: Hamas released three additional hostages on Saturday in exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners as part of the ceasefire deal the terror group reached with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield.
item.dc:creator: Emma Burrows, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: State Department lays out plans for $7 billion-plus arms sale to Israel as Netanyahu visits DC
item.description: WASHINGTON — The State Department has formally told Congress that it plans to sell more than $7 billion in weapons to Israel, including thousands of bombs and missiles, just two days after President Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House.
item.dc:creator: By LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: President Trump says 'we will have relations with North Korea'; it's a 'big asset' that he gets along with Kim
item.description: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba at the White House and touted his good relationship with Kim Jong Un from meetings in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump delayed tariffs on low-cost packages from China
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order this week that temporarily prevents low-cost packages from China from being slapped with his new tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu: 'No future' in Gaza unless 'Hamas destroyed' amid concern for hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Hamas must be 'destroyed,' following discussions with President Trump and lawmakers on the Hill, despite concerns that hard rhetoric could jeopardize hostage releases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Western backers will meet for arms talks as doubts over US intentions grow
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The main international forum for drumming up weapons and ammunition for Ukraine will for the first time meet under the auspices of a country other than the U.S. as uncertainty surrounds the future of Washington’s support for arming the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sanctions on ICC protects US military and government officials, former NSC staffer says
item.description: Amid the International Criminal Court’s issuance of arrest warrants for Israeli leaders for their role in waging a self-defense war against Hamas, President Donald Trump punished the global body, prompting strong reactions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: São Paulo, Brazil small plane crash kills 2
item.description: A small plane crashed into a bus on a road in São Paulo, Brazil, on Friday, killing the pilot and co-pilot and injuring two people on the ground, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia claims it's taken another eastern Ukraine town. Kyiv officials make no comment
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed on Friday that its forces had captured the coal mining town of Toretsk in their latest breakthrough in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region, where Ukrainian defenses are creaking.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A small plane loses altitude after takeoff and slams into a Sao Paulo avenue, killing 2 passengers
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A small aircraft crashed on an avenue in Sao Paulo on Friday morning shortly after taking off from a nearby private airport, killing at least two of its passengers, the local firefighter corps said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and 'no one else' can end the Ukraine-Russia war, US ally says
item.description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjártó told Fox News Digital that President Donald Trump has the necessary credibility with both Ukraine and Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffries amps up call for two-state solution after Trump push to seize Gaza
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Wednesday flatly rejected President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take over the Gaza Strip, suggesting it would undermine ongoing efforts to free Israeli hostages and bring peace to the volatile region.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Saudis could 'de-radicalize' Palestinians: Trump envoy
item.description: “We’ll have a lot of clarity for you in the coming days,” a Trump envoy says of questions about Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
